name: Water
notes: Universal solvent base for cosmetics.

name: Glycerin
notes: Draws moisture to the skin; well-tolerated.

name: Niacinamide
notes: Reduces hyperpigmentation and improves barrier function.

name: Hyaluronic Acid
notes: Holds up to 1000x its weight in water; deeply hydrating.

name: Tocopherol
notes: Protects skin from free radical damage.

name: Dimethicone
notes: Silicone that smooths and protects skin.

name: Cyclopentasiloxane
notes: Lightweight silicone carrier; evaporates quickly.

name: Butylene Glycol
notes: Conditioning agent that also enhances product texture.

name: Cetearyl Alcohol
notes: Fatty alcohol used as an emollient and thickener; not a drying alcohol.

name: Titanium Dioxide
notes: Physical sunscreen agent; may cause flashback in photography.

name: Zinc Oxide
notes: Physical sunscreen agent; may leave a white cast.

name: Squalane
notes: Lightweight oil that mimics skin's natural lipids.

name: Panthenol
notes: Moisturizes and soothes irritated skin.

name: Aloe Barbadensis Leaf Juice
notes: Anti-inflammatory botanical extract.

name: Phenoxyethanol
notes: Common preservative; generally well-tolerated.

name: Cetyl Alcohol
notes: Fatty alcohol used to thicken and stabilize formulas.

name: Stearic Acid
notes: Naturally occurring fatty acid used to thicken products.

name: Caprylic/Capric Triglyceride
notes: Coconut-derived oil that softens skin.

name: Ethylhexylglycerin
notes: Deodorant and preservative enhancer.

name: Shea Butter
notes: Rich moisturizer from shea tree nuts.

name: Parfum
notes: Umbrella term for scent compounds; common sensitizer and top contact allergen.

name: Limonene
notes: Citrus-derived fragrance allergen; must be declared above 0.001% in leave-on products.

name: Linalool
notes: Floral fragrance component; oxidizes to form skin sensitizers.

name: Citronellol
notes: Rose-scented fragrance allergen.

name: Geraniol
notes: Sweet floral fragrance allergen; potent sensitizer.

name: Coumarin
notes: Vanilla-like fragrance compound; EU-regulated allergen.

name: Methylparaben
notes: Paraben preservative; potential endocrine disruptor and contact allergen.

name: Propylparaben
notes: Paraben preservative with higher concern due to chain length.

name: Butylparaben
notes: Long-chain paraben with stronger estrogenic activity.

name: Ethylparaben
notes: Short-chain paraben preservative.

name: Isobutylparaben
notes: Branched paraben variant with sensitizing potential.

name: Sodium Lauryl Sulfate
notes: Strong anionic surfactant; can strip natural oils and irritate skin.

name: Sodium Laureth Sulfate
notes: Milder than SLS but can still irritate sensitive skin with prolonged exposure.

name: Ammonium Lauryl Sulfate
notes: Sulfate surfactant similar in irritation potential to SLS.

name: Ammonium Laureth Sulfate
notes: Sulfate-based cleanser; can be drying for sensitive skin types.

name: Sodium Coco-Sulfate
notes: Coconut-derived sulfate; marketed as gentler but still a sulfate.

name: FD&C Yellow No. 5
notes: Azo dye linked to hypersensitivity reactions, especially in aspirin-sensitive individuals.

name: FD&C Red No. 40
notes: Widely used azo dye; potential allergen and irritant.

name: FD&C Blue No. 1
notes: Synthetic triphenylmethane dye; can cause contact sensitization.

name: D&C Red No. 6
notes: Common lip colorant; may cause irritation in sensitive individuals.

name: D&C Red No. 7
notes: Calcium lake of Red 6; used extensively in lip products.

name: FD&C Yellow No. 6
notes: Azo dye that may cause allergic reactions in some individuals.

name: Lanolin
notes: Sheep wool-derived wax; common contact allergen causing eczematous reactions.

name: Lanolin Alcohol
notes: Derivative of lanolin; retains allergenic potential.

name: Lanolin Oil
notes: Refined lanolin fraction; lower but still present allergen risk.

name: Acetylated Lanolin
notes: Chemically modified lanolin; may still trigger sensitivity.

name: Hydrogenated Lanolin
notes: Processed lanolin with reduced but not eliminated allergen potential.

name: Nickel
notes: Metal contaminant in cosmetics; #1 cause of contact dermatitis worldwide.

name: CI 77510
notes: Blue pigment that may contain trace nickel impurities.

name: Iron Oxides
notes: Common pigments that can contain trace nickel as a manufacturing impurity.

name: Chromium Oxide Greens
notes: Green pigment that may contain trace nickel.

name: DMDM Hydantoin
notes: Formaldehyde-releasing preservative; known sensitizer.

name: Imidazolidinyl Urea
notes: Releases formaldehyde over time; common allergen in patch testing.

name: Diazolidinyl Urea
notes: Formaldehyde donor; more potent releaser than imidazolidinyl urea.

name: Quaternium-15
notes: Strongest formaldehyde releaser among cosmetic preservatives.

name: Bronopol
notes: Formaldehyde releaser; may also form nitrosamines.

name: Sodium Hydroxymethylglycinate
notes: Amino acid-derived formaldehyde releaser.

name: Lavandula Angustifolia Oil
notes: Contains linalool and linalyl acetate; photoallergenic and irritating at high concentrations.

name: Lavandula Hybrida Oil
notes: Lavandin hybrid oil with similar allergen profile to true lavender oil.

name: Lavandula Stoechas Extract
notes: Lavender species extract; can cause contact sensitization.

name: Lavender Water
notes: Distillation by-product of lavender; dilute but still contains allergens.

name: Mentha Piperita Oil
notes: Contains menthol and pulegone; can cause contact dermatitis and burning.

name: Menthol
notes: Peppermint derivative; irritant at high concentrations, especially on sensitive skin.

name: Mentha Piperita Leaf Extract
notes: Peppermint extract with potential for skin sensitization.

name: Mentha Arvensis Leaf Oil
notes: Wild mint oil; high menthol content makes it a strong potential irritant.

name: Melaleuca Alternifolia Leaf Oil
notes: Oxidizes rapidly into potent allergens; increasing contact allergy rates globally.

name: Melaleuca Alternifolia Extract
notes: Tea tree-derived extract; milder but shares oxidation-related sensitizing compounds.

name: Melaleuca Leucadendra Oil
notes: Related Melaleuca species; cross-reacts with tea tree oil allergens.

name: Tea Tree Leaf Water
notes: Dilute tea tree distillate; lower potency but retains sensitizing terpenes.

name: Retinol
notes: Vitamin A derivative; can cause initial irritation but is not an allergen.

name: Salicylic Acid
notes: BHA exfoliant; effective for acne-prone skin.

name: Glycolic Acid
notes: AHA exfoliant that promotes cell turnover.

name: Ascorbic Acid
notes: Potent antioxidant for brightening and collagen synthesis.

name: Ceramide NP
notes: Skin-identical lipid that restores barrier function.

name: Centella Asiatica Extract
notes: Anti-inflammatory botanical; popular in K-beauty for calming redness.

name: Allantoin
notes: Gentle skin protectant that promotes healing.

name: Mica
notes: Natural mineral pigment that adds shimmer and opacity.

name: Talc
notes: Mineral powder used for oil absorption and slip.

name: Kaolin
notes: Natural clay that absorbs excess oil.

name: Jojoba Oil
notes: Wax ester similar to skin's natural sebum.

name: Rosehip Oil
notes: Rich in essential fatty acids and vitamin A.

name: Benzyl Alcohol
notes: Preservative and fragrance component; EU-listed allergen.

name: Cinnamal
notes: Cinnamon-derived fragrance; potent contact sensitizer.

name: Eugenol
notes: Clove-derived fragrance allergen; EU-regulated.

name: Coconut Oil
notes: Highly moisturizing plant oil, but known to be highly comedogenic (pore-clogging).

name: Cocoa Butter
notes: Rich emollient butter, highly comedogenic.

name: Argan Oil
notes: Nourishing oil rich in antioxidants and fatty acids.

name: Isopropyl Myristate
notes: Synthetic emollient that improves spreadability, highly comedogenic.

name: Isopropyl Palmitate
notes: Thickening agent and emollient, highly comedogenic.

name: Wheat Germ Oil
notes: Nutrient-rich oil, highly comedogenic.

name: Linseed Oil
notes: Flax-derived oil rich in omega-3, highly comedogenic.

name: Silica
notes: Mineral powder that absorbs sweat and oil, improving product slip.

name: Zinc
notes: Helps control sebum production and has anti-inflammatory properties.

name: Propylene Glycol
notes: Common humectant that helps skin retain moisture and aids ingredient penetration.

name: Sodium Benzoate
notes: Common food and cosmetic preservative, highly effective against fungi.

name: Potassium Sorbate
notes: Mild preservative used as a paraben alternative.

name: Acrylates Copolymer
notes: Synthetic polymer used to stabilize emulsions and create a water-resistant film.

name: Polyethylene Glycol
notes: Broad family of polymers used to bind water and help mix oil and water.
